description: Phase 8.1 evaluation design: functional_evaluation_plan, quality_evaluation_plan (per-NFR), reasoning_evaluation_plan with grounded FR/NFR id citations.

template_variables.active_constraints:
1. 100% correctness and completeness — always. All three layers of correctness (Section 1.6) are required. (Authority 7, source: 4394e1a8-f6d1-42c0-a25c-7991e6d02a5e)
2. Every phase is mandatory and executed in order. The Orchestrator cannot skip phases. (Authority 7, source: 0450a160-f9ec-4e41-ab94-188f37bf1794)
3. All Artifacts are owned by JanumiCode and stored in the Governed Stream database. (Authority 7, source: a1030128-7334-4d7a-8ae9-8900de3c2993)
4. Prompt Templates use namespace prefixing ([JC:] and [P:]) and separate context scopes at all times. (Authority 7, source: a67d4502-972e-4c08-b482-19fbc402a5e9)
5. The Governed Stream is lossless. All execution trace content — reasoning steps, self-corrections, tool call invocations, tool results — is captured in full regardless of what subset is used for any given downstream purpose. (Authority 7, source: 55a61a95-64fd-475b-a985-7962936fb07e)
6. No governing constraint may be truncated silently. Governing constraints (Authority Level 6+) are always delivered in full via the stdin directive channel. (Authority 7, source: b680da1d-f6d6-4113-ab4d-1a166154aff7)
7. The Governed Stream is single-threaded. No parallel Workflow Runs in a Workspace. (Authority 7, source: 5c77f777-a698-405b-9881-b6813ae04ec5)
8. Agents never exercise judgment. Judgment is always escalated to the human. (Authority 7, source: b3e3ec17-574d-4915-99a2-244e0e71639e)
9. Every human interaction is recorded in the Governed Stream in full detail. (Authority 7, source: d7f5ea89-7265-4416-847f-348290ea28a2)
10. Every Phase Gate requires human approval. No automated gate passage. (Authority 7, source: ba84c364-c2f2-4a63-86ff-f3a49aba6c19)
template_variables.functional_requirements_summary:
16 User Stories:
  US-001 [critical]: As a Link Sharer, I want to Submit a long URL to generate a unique 6-char slug via POST endpoint, so that System stores encrypted original URL and assigns a unique VOC-SHORT-URL slug for immediate use
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-001: POST /shortener with a valid URL payload returns HTTP 201 and includes a VOC-SHORT-URL field with a 6-character alphanumeric slug, with redirect resolution to the target URL occurring within VV-REDIRECT-LATENCY threshold
    AC-002: Stored mapping of VOC-ORIGINAL-URL to VOC-SHORT-URL contains ciphertext values in primary storage, with no plaintext URL found in storage logs or VV-SECURITY-ENCRYPTION verification
    AC-003: POST /shortener with missing, empty, or malformed URL field returns HTTP 400 with error code INVALID_URL and no record is persisted
    AC-004: POST /shortener generates a unique slug for each request, and no collision occurs where two different VOC-ORIGINAL-URLs map to the same VOC-SHORT-URL slug
    AC-005: DELETE /shortener/{slug} using the created VOC-SHORT-URL successfully removes the mapping from storage, confirming COMP-PII-SANCTION deletion obligation support
  US-002 [critical]: As a Link Clicker, I want to Navigate to a VOC-SHORT-URL slug to access original destination, so that System validates slug existence and securely redirects to VOC-ORIGINAL-URL
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-001: GET /shorten/{slug} for a valid VOC-SHORT-URL returns HTTP 302 with Location header containing the VOC-ORIGINAL-URL
    AC-002: HTTP 302 returned with Location header pointing to decrypted VOC-ORIGINAL-URL
    AC-003: GET /shorten/{slug} where slug not found in mapping returns HTTP 404 with error code SLUG_NOT_FOUND
    AC-004: If decryption metadata fails validation, system returns HTTP 400 with error code DECRYPTION_INVALID and no redirect occurs
    AC-005: 95% of redirect requests complete within 100ms as per UJ-CLICK-SHORT-URL acceptance criteria
    AC-006: audit-log entry with action='redirect.issue' is written before HTTP response is flushed
  US-003 [high]: As a API Client, I want to Submit batch POST requests for multiple link creation, so that System queues and processes requests returning status codes for each link
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-001: POST /links with valid payload returns HTTP 200 OK and response body is array of VOC-SHORT-URL objects
    AC-002: All VOC-SLUG values returned in batch response are distinct and not duplicated within the same batch
    AC-003: POST request exceeds ENT-RATE-LIMIT-BUCKET threshold returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
    AC-004: Batch response includes HTTP 400 Bad Request for specific items containing invalid original_url while processing valid items
    AC-005: GET /links/{slug} returns HTTP 200 with stored VOC-ORIGINAL-URL and associated metadata
  US-004 [high]: As a API Client, I want to Initiate request that exceeds configured request rate, so that System blocks excess traffic and enforces DoS protection
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-004: POST request exceeding ENT-RATE-LIMIT-CONFIG threshold returns HTTP 429 with Retry-After header value matching the remaining reset window duration
    AC-005: HTTP 429 response is returned without persisting the request body or triggering downstream workflow steps in ENT-RATE-LIMIT-CONFIG scope
    AC-006: An audit-log entry is written before HTTP response is flushed containing client identifier and action='rate.limit_exceeded'
    AC-007: Requests received after the configured reset duration elapse return HTTP 200 with the standard response body without triggering a 429
    AC-008: Updates to ENT-RATE-LIMIT-CONFIG threshold values are effective for subsequent requests without manual restart of the enforcement service
  US-005 [critical]: As a Link Sharer (EU), I want to Request data export for VOC-SHORT-URLs under GDPR Right to Access, so that System compiles and delivers file containing all associated stored data
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-005: POST /export-gdpr returns HTTP 200 and response payload lists all VOC-SHORT-URL records with original_url, created_at, and click_count fields
    AC-006: Response includes compliance_header object where jurisdiction equals EU and regulatory_basis equals GDPR for EU users
    AC-007: Request for user with no VOC-SHORT-URL records returns HTTP 200 with empty array payload rather than HTTP 404
    AC-008: Audit log entry with actor_id, action=export_request, and timestamp is written to ENT-COMPLIANCE-DATA before HTTP response is flushed
    AC-009: exported_at timestamp on the generated file is within the defined compliance processing window relative to request_timestamp
  US-006 [critical]: As a Link Sharer, I want to Invoke deletion request for a specific VOC-SHORT-URL, so that System purges data from storage and cache per COMP-PII-SANCTION
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-006: POST /delete/VOC-SHORT-URL/{slug} returns 200 OK and GET /shortener/{slug} returns 404 within 1 second
    AC-007: POST /delete/VOC-SHORT-URL/{slug} returns 403 FORBIDDEN when the actor_id does not match the owner of the VOC-SLUG
    AC-008: ENT-LIFECYCLE-LOG contains a new entry with action='delete' and actor_id matching the requester immediately after operation completion
    AC-009: ENT-GDPR-REQUEST is updated to status='fulfilled' with the deletion timestamp matching the VOC-SHORT-URL deletion event
  US-007 [high]: As a Marketing Manager, I want to Access analytics dashboard to view VOC-CLICK-COUNTER metrics, so that System displays campaign statistics to authorized users only
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-007: GET /dashboard/campaigns/{id} returns 200 and the returned campaign statistics object matches ENT-CAMPAIGN-STAT and includes geo-region mapping from ENT-IP-GEO-DATA for recorded IPs
    AC-008: GET /dashboard/campaigns/{id} from a user without Marketing Manager role returns HTTP 403 with error code ACCESS_DENIED
    AC-009: GET /dashboard/campaigns/{id} returns the geo-list with 'Unknown Region' where ENT-IP-GEO-DATA has no mapping for the specific IP instead of failing the request
    AC-010: GET /dashboard/campaigns/{id} returns response within the time bound specified by VV-REDIRECT-LATENCY system constant
  US-008 [high]: As a Marketing Manager, I want to Create a tracking link with campaign metadata attached, so that Link functions as VOC-TINYURL for marketing tracking purposes
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-001: POST /campaigns/link returns HTTP 201 with payload containing VOC-SLUG (6-char), VOC-ORIGINAL-URL, and campaign_id reference
    AC-002: GET /{VOC-SLUG} returns HTTP 302 with Location header equal to stored VOC-ORIGINAL-URL
    AC-003: POST /campaigns/link with malformed URL returns HTTP 400 with error code INVALID_URL
    AC-004: GET /campaigns/{id}/links lists the link and its associated campaign_id in the response payload
    AC-005: GET /link/{slug}/stats returns VOC-CLICK-COUNTER incremented value after at least one VOC-ORIGINAL-URL click is recorded
    AC-006: Generated VOC-SLUG is a unique 6-character string consisting of alphanumeric characters only
  US-009 [high]: As a Platform Ops, I want to Review system health and availability metrics, so that System reports operational status and latency targets
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-009: GET /health returns HTTP 200 and response JSON contains `status` field set to `healthy` when the service is operational
    AC-010: The `latency` field in the response JSON contains a numeric value representing milliseconds that is <= limit defined in VV-REDIRECT-LATENCY
    AC-011: When the service is unavailable, GET /health returns HTTP 503 with response body containing `status` field set to `unavailable`
    AC-012: Each GET /health request generates an audit-log entry with action='health.check' and timestamp matching the request time
  US-010 [critical]: As a Platform Ops, I want to Initiate disaster recovery plan upon regional failure detection, so that System restores service availability and verifies data integrity
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-010: GET /health/status returns service_state='restored' within 15 minutes of trigger_event_timestamp AND data_integrity_check_result equals 'passed'
    AC-011: GET /disaster-state returns current_state='active' before HTTP response is flushed to the client
    AC-012: POST /disaster-recovery/initiate returns HTTP 409 with error_code='RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE' if ENT-RECOVERY-PLAN.backup_endpoint_availability is false
    AC-013: Audit log entry with action='disaster-recovery-initiate' and actor_id='PlatformOps' is persisted before HTTP response is flushed
  US-011 [high]: As a Link Sharer, I want to Attempt access via HTTP endpoint for VOC-TINYURL link, so that System enforces SECURE transport and redirects to HTTPS
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-011: GET /{slug} over HTTP returns HTTP 301 or 302 with Location header pointing to HTTPS URL
    AC-012: GET /{slug} over HTTPS returns HTTP 200 OK and serves the redirected content
    AC-013: HTTP request bodies are not logged or processed in cleartext by the application logic
    AC-014: Redirect response chain depth does not exceed one hop and no subsequent HTTP redirects are issued
  US-012 [critical]: As a Link Sharer, I want to Submit a known malicious or phishing VOC-ORIGINAL-URL, so that System blocks submission and prevents malicious storage
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-012: POST /submit-url with a VOC-ORIGINAL-URL matching ENT-THREAT-INDICATOR patterns returns HTTP 403 Forbidden; no VOC-SHORT-URL mapping record is persisted in the database
    AC-013: POST /submit-url with a VOC-ORIGINAL-URL that does not match ENT-THREAT-INDICATOR patterns returns HTTP 201 Created and persists a VOC-SHORT-URL mapping record
    AC-014: HTTP 403 response includes a 'message' JSON field containing the text 'blocked' or 'security filter'
    AC-015: An audit-log entry with action='block.url', status='rejected', and reason referencing ENT-THREAT-INDICATOR is written before the HTTP 403 response is flushed
    AC-016: The system queries ENT-SCAN-RESULT for the VOC-ORIGINAL-URL; if ENT-SCAN-RESULT indicates 'MALICIOUS', the POST request is rejected with HTTP 403; if null, the URL passes security check unless pattern matches ENT-THREAT-INDICATOR
  US-013 [medium]: As a Marketing Manager, I want to Receive notification when VOC-POLICY-RULE dictates data expiry, so that User is informed before data retention window closes
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-013: POST /notifications endpoint returns status 201 and triggers the email/API channel within 24 hours of the retention expiry timestamp defined in ENT-POLICY-RULE
    AC-014: Notification payload contains the VOC-SHORT-URL string that corresponds to the URL stored in the ENT-POLICY-RULE
    AC-015: Notification payload includes the ENT-POLICY-RULE ID and the VOC-ORIGINAL-URL destination
    AC-016: System writes an audit-log entry with action='notify.expiry' linking the notification event ID to the ENT-POLICY-RULE ID
  US-014 [high]: As a Platform Ops, I want to Query system audit logs for debugging or compliance, so that Access to immutable JSON audit trail for ENT-AUDIT-RECORD
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-014: GET /audit returns HTTP 200 and response body contains JSON object with fields: timestamp, actor_id, action, resource_id
    AC-015: PUT /audit/{id} returns HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed to ensure integrity is maintained on existing entries
    AC-016: GET /audit returns entries where each timestamp field value is consistent with the System Clock reference and monotonically increasing within the same stream
    AC-017: GET /audit?start_date=YYYY-MM-DD&end_date=YYYY-MM-DD returns only records with timestamps falling strictly within the requested date range
    AC-018: GET /audit?date=invalid returns HTTP 400 Bad Request with error code QUERY_INVALID_PARAMETER
  US-015 [critical]: As a Platform Ops, I want to Perform storage integrity and key rotation checks, so that Stored URLs remain encrypted and accessible for decryption
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-001: SELECT FROM storage.raw_tables WHERE id=RETURNED_ID returns encrypted blob only; no fields contain readable Original URL strings
    AC-002: Decryption call using current valid key returns Original URL with 200 OK; corrupted blobs return 400
    AC-003: ENT-COMMIT-LOG query returns entry with action=key_rotation AND new_key_id references VOC-ENCRYPTED-PAYLOAD metadata
    AC-004: Payload metadata header indicates AES-256 and block size is 128-bit compatible per COMP-AES-256-REST
    AC-005: Attempt to decrypt with stale or invalid key returns error code DECRYPTION_KEY_MISMATCH before data is returned
  US-016 [medium]: As a Link Clicker, I want to Attempt to access link that has triggered timeout condition, so that User receives timeout notification without service crash
    Acceptance Criteria:
    AC-016: GET /short-url/{slug} returns HTTP 408 Request Timeout or HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout and response body contains string 'timeout'
    AC-017: ENT-REDIRECT-TIMEOUT entity record is created in primary storage with timestamp and short_url_id
    AC-018: GET /health-check returns HTTP 200 OK and status is healthy immediately after timeout event
    AC-019: Subsequent requests to same short URL returns identical HTTP 408 response and no unhandled exception is recorded in system logs
template_variables.non_functional_requirements_summary:
15 NFRs:
  NFR-001 [performance]: Redirect endpoint latency P95 under nominal load — threshold: P95 latency for the redirect endpoint must be ≤ 100 ms over any rolling 5-minute window
  NFR-002 [availability]: Monthly uptime for redirect endpoint including planned maintenance — threshold: Monthly uptime ≥ 99.9% over rolling 30-day windows, inclusive of planned maintenance windows.
  NFR-003 [availability]: Recovery Time Objective on full regional failure — threshold: Recovery Time Objective (RTO) on full regional failure is ≤ 15 minutes.
  NFR-004 [security]: Original URLs stored encrypted at rest using AES-256 — threshold: Zero plaintext occurrence of any submitted URL in direct read of underlying Postgres data files; AES-256 cipher suite mandatory for all stored payload columns.
  NFR-005 [observability]: Structured JSON logs to stdout ingested by platform — threshold: All logs conform to structured JSON and stream to stdout; audit logs retained for ≥ 365 days; ingestion failure alerts triggered within < 1 minute.
  NFR-006 [security]: HTTPS enforced on all public endpoints with HSTS headers — threshold: HTTP requests must redirect to HTTPS; HTTPS only on all public endpoints; HSTS headers enabled; SSL/TLS 1.2 or higher required.
  NFR-007 [reliability]: Every stored slug in the mappings table must be unique — threshold: SELECT slug, COUNT(*) FROM url_mappings GROUP BY slug HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 returns zero rows
  NFR-008 [compliance]: Deletion requests from Sharers purged from storage and cache — threshold: 100% of valid deletion requests from Sharers result in full purging from persistent storage and ephemeral cache within 24 hours of receipt; zero retention beyond this window.
  NFR-009 [compliance]: GDPR data export for VOC-SHORT-URLs under Right to Access — threshold: Export file contains 100% of stored link data associated with the requesting user; zero data loss or omission allowed.
  NFR-010 [reliability]: Daily automated backup execution and backup replication integrity — threshold: 100% of daily scheduled backup jobs execute successfully; zero replication integrity errors detected.
  NFR-011 [security]: Threat feed blocking latency and IP reputation scoring — threshold: Threat indicator blocking latency ≤ 5 seconds for all valid requests; IP reputation scoring accuracy ≥ 95% on validation queries; Threat feed update frequency ≤ 15 minutes.
  NFR-012 [scalability]: Rate limit enforced at 1000 requests per minute per IP — threshold: 1000 requests per IP address per rolling 60-second window; excess requests rejected with HTTP 429 status code.
  NFR-013 [security]: Malicious URL submission blocked before storage — threshold: Zero malicious URL submissions successfully stored in the content repository.
  NFR-014 [compliance]: Notification when VOC-POLICY-RULE dictates data expiry — threshold: Notification attempt generated for every policy-expiry event before the expiry timestamp; zero missed notifications for active retention records.
  NFR-015 [reliability]: Handling timeout condition without service crash — threshold: Zero service crashes triggered by timeout conditions.
template_variables.test_plan_summary:
(read-only — do not duplicate test cases)
7 Test Suites:
  SEC-VAL-GW-001 (unit, component: sec-validation-gateway):
    TC-SEC-VAL-01 [functional]: traces to ["AC-SEC-VAL-01","AC-SEC-VAL-03"], expected: System rejects malicious payload in request body and returns HTTP 400 Bad Request within 500ms latency.
    TC-SEC-VAL-02 [functional]: traces to ["AC-SEC-VAL-02","AC-SEC-VAL-04"], expected: HTTP 301 Redirect is returned with HSTS header for non-HTTPS requests; Rate limit of 1000 req/IP/60s is enforced.
    TC-SEC-VAL-03 [functional]: traces to ["AC-SEC-VAL-05","AC-SEC-VAL-04"], expected: Malformed URL submissions are rejected at storage interface with event logged; 403 Forbidden response returned.
  LINK-MAP-REP-001 (integration, component: link-mapping-repository):
    TC-LINK-REP-01 [functional]: traces to ["AC-LINK-REP-01","AC-LINK-REP-03"], expected: Valid slug returns encrypted payload metadata and target destination URL; Unique constraint violation returned for duplicate slug.
    TC-LINK-REP-02 [functional]: traces to ["AC-LINK-REP-02","AC-LINK-REP-01"], expected: New URL records stored as encrypted blobs; Direct database reads do not display plaintext URL values.
  LINK-LIF-PROC-001 (integration, component: link-lifecycle-processor):
    TC-LIFE-PROC-01 [functional]: traces to ["AC-LIFE-PROC-01"], expected: Valid deletion requests are purged from persistent storage and ephemeral cache within the 24-hour window.
    TC-LIFE-PROC-02 [functional]: traces to ["AC-LIFE-PROC-02"], expected: Export job returns 100% of stored link data for requested user identity; Zero missing records in export payload.
    TC-LIFE-PROC-03 [functional]: traces to ["AC-LIFE-PROC-03"], expected: Notification attempts are triggered for every policy-expiry event before the expiry timestamp.
  PLAT-OBS-001 (unit, component: platform-observability):
    TC-OBS-01 [functional]: traces to ["AC-PLATFORM-OB-01"], expected: Latency metrics exposed to Prometheus with P95 histograms; Alerts configuration loaded successfully.
    TC-OBS-02 [functional]: traces to ["AC-PLATFORM-OB-02"], expected: Synthetic probes executed according to monthly schedule; Uptime metrics aggregated for reporting.
  PLAT-AUDIT-001 (integration, component: platform-audit):
    TC-AUDIT-01 [functional]: traces to ["AC-PLATFORM-LOG-01"], expected: Logs emitted as JSON objects to stdout with ISO8601 timestamps; Audit data retained for 365 days.
  PLAT-DR-001 (end_to_end, component: platform-disaster-recovery):
    TC-DR-01 [functional]: traces to ["AC-PLATFORM-DR-01"], expected: Regional failure events ingested and mapped correctly; Recovery trigger events emitted immediately.
    TC-DR-02 [functional]: traces to ["AC-PLATFORM-DR-02"], expected: Backup replication jobs complete without integrity errors; Replicated data matches source data at the byte level.
  PLAT-FT-001 (integration, component: platform-fault-tolerance):
    TC-FT-01 [functional]: traces to ["AC-PLATFORM-FT-01"], expected: Timeout conditions handled without triggering service crashes; Graceful termination of long-running requests.

criteria.success_condition: GET /dashboard/campaigns/{id} returns HTTP 403 and error code ACCESS_DENIED for users without Marketing Manager role, ensuring dashboard visibility is restricted strictly by role.
GET /health/status returns service_state='restored' within the 15-minute threshold after trigger_event_timestamp, and data_integrity_check_result equals 'passed'.
GET /{slug} over HTTP returns HTTP 301/302 redirecting to HTTPS, and GET /{slug} over HTTPS returns HTTP 200 OK without cleartext body logging.
SELECT FROM storage.raw_tables WHERE id=RETURNED_ID returns encrypted blob only; Decryption with valid key succeeds, and decryption with stale key fails with DECRYPTION_KEY_MISMATCH.
GET /short-url/{slug} for timed-out links returns HTTP 408/504 with 'timeout' body, and subsequent requests return identical responses without service crashes.

criteria.fallback_if_tool_unavailable: Manual endpoint latency sampling via CLI

criteria.measurement_method: Aggregated uptime calculation excluding planned maintenance
Comparison of failover timestamp to service restoration timestamp

criteria.threshold: Zero plaintext URL occurrences in Postgres files
criteria.measurement_method: Binary search for plaintext URL patterns in data files
criteria.fallback_if_tool_unavailable: Direct inspection of storage logs for plaintext strings

criteria.measurement_method: Log parser validation and retention policy verification

criteria.threshold: HTTP redirects to HTTPS, HSTS headers present, TLS 1.2+
criteria.measurement_method: Network traffic capture and header analysis

criteria.threshold: SELECT slug, COUNT(*) FROM url_mappings GROUP BY slug HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 returns zero
100% of deletion requests purged within 24 hours
criteria.measurement_method: Query mapping table for existence of deleted slugs post-request

criteria.threshold: Export file contains 100% of stored link data; zero omission
criteria.measurement_method: Comparison of exported record count vs database record count per user
criteria.fallback_if_tool_unavailable: Manual audit of export file rows

criteria.threshold: 100% daily backups successful, zero replication errors
criteria.measurement_method: Job status log check and replication hash verification
Timestamp delta between request and block response

criteria.threshold: 1000 requests per IP per 60-second window, excess rejected with 429
criteria.measurement_method: RPS count and HTTP 429 response rate monitoring
Verify storage table contains no entries matching ENT-THREAT-INDICATOR

criteria.threshold: Notification attempt generated before expiry timestamp; zero missed
criteria.measurement_method: Timestamp comparison of policy event vs notification event

criteria.threshold: Zero service crashes triggered by timeout conditions
criteria.measurement_method: System log scan for unhandled exceptions during timeout
criteria.fallback_if_tool_unavailable: Process monitoring via OS supervisor tools

scenarios.description: Verify system behavior when a deletion request (US-006) is received while a read request (US-002) occurs concurrently on the same slug.
scenarios.pass_criteria: Read request returns 404 immediately after deletion commit, with no data consistency errors or race conditions logged.

scenarios.description: Validate system integrity when a key rotation event occurs (US-015) without losing access to historical data.
scenarios.pass_criteria: Decryption with old key fails gracefully, new key succeeds for current data, no data corruption or migration errors.

scenarios.description: Assess system resilience when a GDPR export request (US-005) is initiated against a dataset with no associated links.
scenarios.pass_criteria: System returns HTTP 200 with empty array payload, no 404 error generated, audit log records the export attempt.

baseline.thinking:
The user wants me to design evaluation criteria for Sub-Phase 8.1 (Evaluation Design).
I need to output three JSON objects:
1. `functional_evaluation_plan`
2. `quality_evaluation_plan`
3. `reasoning_evaluation_plan`

I have a set of constraints I must follow.
**Constraint 1: 100% correctness and completeness** - Specifically, every `functional_requirement_id` MUST appear verbatim in `functional_requirements_summary`. Every `nfr_id` MUST appear verbatim in `non_functional_requirements_summary`. No inventing IDs.
**Constraint 2: Test Plan Grounding** - I must NOT duplicate test cases from Phase 7 (Test Plan). I need to identify coverage gaps or add non-functional/quality criteria.
**Constraint 3: NFR Tooling** - Every NFR must have at least one Quality Evaluation criterion with specified tooling (Invariant).
**Constraint 4: Compliance** - Compliance-related NFRs from `compliance_context` must have evaluation criteria. (Note: the input says "Compliance: No compliance regimes" at the bottom, but the User Stories clearly mention GDPR, etc. However, the specific instruction says "No compliance regimes" at the end of the input block, which might contradict the NFRs listed. I need to check the NFRs provided. NFR-008, NFR-009, NFR-014 are compliance-related. I should include them in quality evaluation if they map to NFR IDs provided).

**Input Data Analysis:**
*   **Functional Requirements (User Stories):** US-001 to US-016.
*   **Non-Functional Requirements (NFRs):** NFR-001 to NFR-015.
*   **Test Plan (Phase 7):** 7 Test Suites (SEC-VAL, LINK-MAP, LINK-LIF, PLAT-OBS, PLAT-AUDIT, PLAT-DR, PLAT-FT).
*   **Goal:** Design evaluation criteria NOT already covered by the Test Plan.
*   **Output Format:** JSON objects.

**Analysis of Gaps vs. Test Plan:**
The Test Plan covers specific functional test cases.
- `SEC-VAL-GW`: Security validation (malicious payloads, HSTS, malformed URLs).
- `LINK-MAP-REP`: Link mapping repository (encrypted payloads, unique slugs).
- `LINK-LIF-PROC`: Lifecycle (deletion, export, notifications).
- `PLAT-OBS`: Observability (Prometheus, Synthetic probes).
- `PLAT-AUDIT`: Audit logs (JSON, retention).
- `PLAT-DR`: Disaster recovery (regional failure, backup integrity).
- `PLAT-FT`: Fault tolerance (timeouts).

I need to ensure that the `quality_evaluation_plan` covers every NFR with specific tooling, and `functional_evaluation_plan` covers functional requirements not fully covered by the Test Plan cases (or supplementing them). However, the prompt says "Design evaluation criteria for quality attributes NOT already covered by the Test Plan."
But I also need to ensure NFRs (NFR-001 to NFR-015) have quality criteria.
The prompt says "Receive the test_plan as read-only input to ensure no duplication."
It also says "No evaluation criterion should duplicate a Test Case from Phase 7".
So I need to look at the NFRs and see if their evaluation is covered by the Phase 7 Test Plan cases.
Actually, the Test Plan cases are `TC-...`. They trace to Acceptance Criteria (AC).
I need to check if the *evaluation* of these NFRs is covered by the Test Plan.
The Test Plan says "TC-SEC-VAL-01... traces to AC-SEC-VAL-01...".
Wait, the ACs in the input are labeled like "AC-001", "AC-002" etc within each US. They are not labeled "AC-SEC-VAL-01".
The Test Plan references like "AC-SEC-VAL-01" seem to be generic labels in the Test Plan description, while the User Stories have "AC-001", "AC-002"...
I need to be careful not to duplicate the *Test Cases* (TC-...), but I need to create *Quality Evaluation Criteria* (NFR based) and *Functional Evaluation Criteria* (FR based).
Since the Test Plan is Phase 7, and this is Phase 8.1 (Evaluation Design), the criteria I design are the *planning* for the evaluation metrics (often non-functional), whereas the Test Plan covers the *functional* verification.
However, NFRs are Quality attributes. The Test Plan includes `PLAT-OBS-01`, `PLAT-DR-01` which cover observability and disaster recovery.
The instruction "Design evaluation criteria for quality attributes NOT already covered by the Test Plan" suggests I should focus on the Quality/Non-Functional Evaluation, but I also need to output `functional_evaluation_plan`.
Usually, Functional Evaluation aligns with User Stories, Quality Evaluation aligns with NFRs.
The instruction "No evaluation criterion should duplicate a Test Case from Phase 7" applies to the criteria themselves.
The Test Plan provides functional verification cases. I should ensure `functional_evaluation_plan` does not just copy those cases but perhaps defines the evaluation logic or criteria derived from the User Stories for the evaluation phase, OR it focuses on the FRs that weren't explicitly tested in Phase 7 (but looking at the list, Phase 7 covers almost all ACs).
Actually, the `functional_evaluation_plan` is usually derived from the Functional Requirements (US) to define *what* to test functionally. If Phase 7 already has test cases for these ACs, maybe the `functional_evaluation_plan` in Phase 8.1 defines the *criteria* (metrics, conditions) rather than the specific TCs.
However, the constraint "No evaluation criterion should duplicate a Test Case from Phase 7" implies I shouldn't list "POST /shortener with valid payload returns HTTP 201" as a criterion if that is exactly a Test Case.
But `functional_evaluation_plan` expects `{functional_requirement_id, evaluation_method, success_condition}`.
